title: selinux preventing access in named-volumes when container is recreated
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Reusing a named volume results in wrong selinux context of updated file (unconfined_u:object_r:container_ro_file_t:s0) and "Permission denied" when tryingto access the file.

Hope its right here and not a bug in the image :)

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman volume rm pihole-etc pihole-dnsmasq

2. /usr/bin/podman run -d -p 53:53/udp -p 80:80/tcp -p 443:443/tcp --volume pihole-etc:/etc/pihole/:Z --volume pihole-dnsmasq:/etc/dnsmasq.d/:Z --name pihole docker.io/pihole/pihole

3. podman exec -it pihole /usr/bin/tail -1 /etc/pihole/list.0.raw.githubusercontent.com.domains # works!

4. podman stop pihole && podman rm pihole

5. /usr/bin/podman run -d -p 53:53/udp -p 80:80/tcp -p 443:443/tcp --volume pihole-etc:/etc/pihole/:Z --volume pihole-dnsmasq:/etc/dnsmasq.d/:Z --name pihole docker.io/pihole/pihole

6. podman exec -it pihole /usr/bin/tail -1 /etc/pihole/list.0.raw.githubusercontent.com.domains


**Describe the results you received:**
```
[root@fedora system]# podman exec -it pihole /usr/bin/tail -1 /etc/pihole/list.0.raw.githubusercontent.com.domains
/usr/bin/tail: cannot open '/etc/pihole/list.0.raw.githubusercontent.com.domains' for reading: Permission denied
Error: non zero exit code: 1: OCI runtime error
[root@fedora system]#
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
[root@fedora system]# podman exec -it pihole /usr/bin/tail -1 /etc/pihole/list.0.raw.githubusercontent.com.domains
# 0.0.0.0 example.com
[root@fedora system]#
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

In the first "podman run" everything works fine and you can access the file.
But when stoppring & removing the container and recreating its - it fails.
The problem only occures with named volumes, using host-directories is working fine

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:

host:

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  SwapFree: 3439325184

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.8.2-2.fc31.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

* Running in a KVM
* selinux enabled & enforcing
* container runs as root because of the needed ports as DNS server

Logs regardings the issue:
```

comments.body: If you want to share the same volume between two containers you need to use lowercase "z".

Thanks for the fast response.
Its the same result with the lowercase ":z"

```
[root@fedora ~]# podman volume rm pihole-etc pihole-dnsmasq
pihole-etc
pihole-dnsmasq
[root@fedora ~]# /usr/bin/podman run -d -p 53:53/udp -p 80:80/tcp -p 443:443/tcp --volume pihole-etc:/etc/pihole:z --volume pihole-dnsmasq:/etc/dnsmasq.d:z --name pihole docker.io/pihole/pihole
761f549cf67ec9b83216749c167c10451f437d763193c22b632658ff32ca0d68
[root@fedora ~]# podman exec -it pihole /usr/bin/tail -1 /etc/pihole/list.0.raw.githubusercontent.com.domains
# 0.0.0.0 example.com
[root@fedora ~]# podman stop pihole && podman rm pihole
761f549cf67ec9b83216749c167c10451f437d763193c22b632658ff32ca0d68
761f549cf67ec9b83216749c167c10451f437d763193c22b632658ff32ca0d68
[root@fedora ~]# /usr/bin/podman run -d -p 53:53/udp -p 80:80/tcp -p 443:443/tcp --volume pihole-etc:/etc/pihole:z --volume pihole-dnsmasq:/etc/dnsmasq.d:z --name pihole docker.io/pihole/pihole
a114051de6734c8213ea363a43f319f47f69ab28ab0e65a28f3c6a34bf505d85
[root@fedora ~]# podman exec -it pihole /usr/bin/tail -1 /etc/pihole/list.0.raw.githubusercontent.com.domains
/usr/bin/tail: cannot open '/etc/pihole/list.0.raw.githubusercontent.com.domains' for reading: Permission denied
Error: non zero exit code: 1: OCI runtime error
[root@fedora ~]#
```

regards

Is this and SELinux issue?
Does it happen if setenforce 0 is on?
What AVCs are using seeing
ausearch -m avc -ts recent

Here the output from the ausearch command before the "setenforce 0"
```
[root@fedora ~]# ausearch -m avc -ts recent
----
time->Thu Apr  9 22:50:35 2020
type=AVC msg=audit(1586465435.025:3490): avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=82997 comm="bash" name="list.0.raw.githubusercontent.com.domains" dev="dm-0" ino=27914090 sco
ntext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c86,c768 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c55,c689 tclass=file permissive=0
----
time->Thu Apr  9 22:50:35 2020
type=AVC msg=audit(1586465435.025:3491): avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=82997 comm="bash" name="list.1.mirror1.malwaredomains.com.domains" dev="dm-0" ino=27914091 sc
ontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c86,c768 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c55,c689 tclass=file permissive=0
[root@fedora ~]#
```

But it looks that it also happens in permissive mode but as expected it does not block the access - but reports the issue:

```
[root@fedora ~]# podman exec -it pihole /usr/bin/tail -1 /etc/pihole/list.0.raw.githubusercontent.com.domains
# 0.0.0.0 example.com


[root@fedora ~]# ausearch -m avc -ts recent
----
time->Thu Apr  9 23:05:22 2020
type=AVC msg=audit(1586466322.596:3703): avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=88490 comm="bash" name="list.0.raw.githubusercontent.com.domains" dev="dm-0" ino=18819474 sco
ntext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c22,c851 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c716,c1006 tclass=file permissive=1
----
time->Thu Apr  9 23:05:23 2020
type=AVC msg=audit(1586466323.024:3704): avc:  denied  { unlink } for  pid=88516 comm="mv" name="list.0.raw.githubusercontent.com.domains" dev="dm-0" ino=18819474 sco
ntext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c22,c851 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c716,c1006 tclass=file permissive=1
[root@fedora ~]#
´´´

This looks like the original content was not run with "z"  

Lowecase "z" is supposed to set the target label to system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0

Okay, when its a "problem" with the image I will close the issue.
Thanks for your great and super fast support, keep up the great work! :)
